name: Checklist Card
description: Progress-oriented checklist card for onboarding, compliance, and readiness workflows.

onboarding.description: Checklist card with progress bar, prioritized tasks, and one clear next-step action.

slots.header: Title, completion summary, and progress indicator
slots.checklist-item: Single task row with state, label, and helper text
slots.footer-action: Primary next-step action or review button

compact.description: Shorter checklist summary for dashboard side panels.

visual_brief: Single contained card that makes progress obvious and next steps clear. The tone should feel practical and reassuring, with a visible completion signal, a tidy list of tasks, and a clear sense of what's blocking readiness. This is especially important for compliance and onboarding contexts where the card should reduce cognitive load rather than add to it.

responsive.mobile: Full-width stacked card. Task rows wrap helper text underneath cleanly and CTA remains easy to reach.
responsive.tablet: Comfortable card width with visible progress bar and 4-6 checklist items before scrolling becomes necessary.
responsive.desktop: Contained summary card that can sit above tables or beside operational content without overpowering the page.

layout_hints.progress_priority: Lead with the progress summary and next blocking item, then the supporting checklist rows.
layout_hints.task_state: Each row should make completion state obvious through icon, label, and text, not icon color alone.
layout_hints.contextual_role: This pattern is a decision aid. Keep it compact and actionable rather than turning it into a full workflow editor.

motion.micro: Completed items may fade into a calmer state, and progress bars may animate in gently on load.
motion.transitions: Checklist completion should feel affirmative but restrained, especially in enterprise and compliance contexts.

keyboard: Tab through checklist actions and any expandable items in top-to-bottom order.
announcements: Completion state and progress should be announced with task labels so users understand what remains.
